title: Malaysian-Style Fish Fry
description: A flavorful and spicy dish from Malaysia, where fish is stir-fried in a mixture of rich and aromatic spices.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the white fish fillets and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until cooked through.
Remove the fish from the wok and set aside.
In the same wok, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil and stir-fry the stir-fry vegetables until tender.
Add the Malaysian chili paste and stir-fry for another minute.
Return the cooked fish to the wok and pour in the rich soy sauce.
Stir everything together to combine and cook for a further 2-3 minutes.
Season with salt to taste and serve hot.
Serve with steamed rice or noodles.
Garnish with chopped cilantro or scallions, if desired.
